PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard together:
1) PES-20-303698, Estate of Robert D. Harmon; and
2) PTR-25-308449, The Robert Harmon Family Trust

Appearances:
Jodi Phillips appeared via Zoom for Administrator Michelle Pierce, who was also present in the courtroom.
Scott Fraser appeared via Zoom for Petitioner William K. Harmon (via Zoom).
Rosalind Houff, interested party, was also present in the courtroom.

The Court called the matters for hearing on: 1) Petition for Settlement of the First and Final Account; 2) Petition for Probate of Lost Will; 3) Petition for Order Confirming Trust Property; and 4) Case Management Hearing

Ms. Phillips argued that there was no competent witness to verify execution of the will, and therefore it could not be admitted. She maintained that compliance with statutory requirements is mandatory and that clear and convincing evidence of intent was lacking.

Mr. Fraser argued that extrinsic evidence demonstrated the decedent's testamentary intent, citing that the decedent had informed his children of his estate plan, that the drafting attorney customarily ensured wills were executed, and that Michelle Pierce, who discovered the estate planning binder, had both motive and opportunity regarding the absence of the will. He asserted that the presumption of revocation could be overcome under applicable case law.

Ms. Phillips maintained there was no proof the will was ever signed and that the claims of destruction or motive were speculative.

On the trust petition for order confirming trust property, Counsel agreed that the matter could be submitted on the papers in lieu of testimony. The Court confirmed that it had received and reviewed the objections.

Regarding the accounting petition in the estate matter, Ms. Phillips stated that due to new developments, the accounting would need revision. She explained that unresolved issues included a 1/6th interest in the Shields property belonging to the Estate of Diane Robinson, encumbered by a substantial Department of Health Care Services creditor's claim. Mediation efforts to resolve a buyout of that interest had failed, and a partition action may be necessary. Mr. Fraser agreed additional time would be needed and expressed a willingness to work cooperatively.

Following discussion, the Court took the petition for probate of lost will and the trust petition for order confirming trust property under submission. The petition for settlement of the first and final account was continued to February 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The case management hearing was taken off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Aug-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Aug-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jodi Phillips for Michelle A. Pierce, Administrator (via Zoom).
Scott A. Fraser for William K. Harmon, Objector (via Zoom).

The Court acknowledged the parties' Case Management Statement and inquired as to the estimated length of a potential evidentiary hearing. Mr. Fraser estimated two to four days, citing the anticipated number of witnesses, volume of documents, and the duration of Ms. Pierce's deposition. He also informed the Court that he had filed a Petition for Probate of Lost Will under this case number, set for hearing on June 24, 2025, and a separate Heggstad Petition under case number PTR-25-308449, set for August 11, 2025.

Ms. Phillips confirmed she only recently received notice of those filings and intends to file objections to both. She stated that there is no signed copy of the will nor any witness testimony, which she contends invalidates the lost will. She also challenged the sufficiency of evidence in support of the Heggstad Petition.

The Court found that the matter was not yet ready to be set for an evidentiary hearing due to the pending related petitions and incomplete discovery. The Court continued both the June 24 hearing on the Lost Will Petition and the August 11 hearing on the Heggstad petition in the trust case to August 19, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to be heard together. The Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account and Case Management Hearing were continued to be heard on the same date.

The parties were directed to complete discovery and file any objections to the petitions in advance of that date. At the continued hearing, the Court will consider whether to set an evidentiary hearing or explore resolution by other means.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jodi Phillips for Michelle A. Pierce, Petitioner/Administrator (via Zoom).
Scott A. Fraser for William K. Harmon, Objector (via Zoom).

Mr. Fraser asserted the decedent intended for the 650 Shields property to pass via trust, not intestate succession, and suggested discovery or an evidentiary hearing to address unresolved issues. Ms. Phillips contended no pour-over will exists, and the property never became part of the trust. The court noted the issue may be legal, not factual, and questioned the need for an evidentiary hearing. The court took the matter under submission for further review.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
